candidates.why: A separate operating D&A line exists above financial items, but there is no selected COGS parent bucket to which it can be clearly tied. It appears to be part of overall operating expenses rather than specifically cost of sales.
This appears to be a component beneath the broader 'Depreciation and amortization' summary row, so using it would risk double counting. It is also not clearly attributable to COGS.
This is a component of the broader D&A summary and is not clearly COGS-specific.

variables.notes: No COGS row was selected in the earlier mapping, and nothing in the operating-section excerpt identifies a COGS-specific depreciation/amortisation line. Conservative choice is to add nothing separately to COGS.

candidates.why: This is a separate operating D&A line above financial items and sits alongside the selected XSGA bucket rows 'Operatingexpenses' and 'Salaries and related expenses'. The arithmetic indicates 'Operating Expense' is the total, while 'Operatingexpenses' + 'Salaries and related expenses' exclude this D&A line, so it should be added separately. It is also the preferred summary row over its components.
This appears to be a component detail under the broader 'Depreciation and amortization' row. Do not select when the summary row is available.
This appears to be a component detail under the broader 'Depreciation and amortization' row. Do not select when the summary row is available.
This looks like a balancing/minor adjustment line, not the primary D&A row to add separately.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA_COMPONENTS bucket is 'Operatingexpenses' plus 'Salaries and related expenses'. The separate 'Depreciation and amortization' row appears outside that bucket at the same operating level, and in at least one year those rows sum to 'Operating Expense', confirming D&A is separate rather than embedded. Use only the summary row to avoid double counting with 'Depreciation' and 'Amortization of Intangibles' components.
